extraction.one_line_summary: ROJAS José Joaquín took leader's jersey after Movistar won the Stage 2 team time trial, though later penalized in Stage 3 for teammate pushing infraction.
tactical_patterns: Movistar unexpectedly fastest in TTT despite not being known for discipline, revealing depth for stage race control.
BMC lost TTT despite specialization, suggesting TJ/Porte GC ambitions compromised early versus Valverde's stronger position.
Sky 46 seconds down in third indicates Froome/Thomas not prioritizing early time gains, banking on mountain superiority.

rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: Movistar built entire TTT around him with fastest time, positioned to control race unlike Quintana's absence suggests
rider_signals.CONTADOR Alberto: negative: Trek 1:15 down in TTT reveals weak support crew for GC defense compared to BMC/Movistar depth
rider_signals.BOUHANNI Nacer: negative: Sprint Stage 1 positioning failure left him mid-pack when Cofidis train collapsed, beaten by lead-out wheel surfer
